mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and consumption demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive correlation between month-over-month PAYEMS changes and 20-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below the 6.0 percent threshold, it signals that labor market slack is diminishing sufficiently to support wage growth and consumer spending without triggering immediate policy tightening, producing positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE is below 6.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signal broad-based demand and supply coordination that reduces recession probability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month beyond the effect of either series alone.
failure_condition: The joint signal from RSAFS and INDPRO produces a hit rate below 55% or a rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the zero lower bound regime, quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP (GDPC1) generates positive S&P 500 quarterly returns because it signals demand recovery without an accompanying central bank policy rate response, lifting earnings expectations.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, positive surprises in GDPC1 coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing below the one million unit threshold indicate a contraction in residential investment and forward-looking construction employment, leading to negative S&P 500 monthly returns as the housing multiplier fades.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1000, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months, refuting the contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving capacity utilization and operating leverage for goods-producing firms, generating positive one-week S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between monthly INDPRO changes and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is not positive and significant (p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero, month-over-month increases in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) jointly signal an imminent shift to tightening, and this interaction produces negative S&P 500 monthly returns because the inflation surprise is not offset by higher current nominal rates.
failure_condition: In months where both FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL rises, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in retail sales (RSAFS) signal a sustained deterioration in consumer demand that precedes inventory corrections and earnings downgrades, generating negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Following two consecutive monthly declines in RSAFS, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.
